summary: date-fns-lite exfiltrates installer identity via DNS beacon on postinstall
details: date-fns-lite@1.0.1 is a typosquat of the widely-used date-fns package. On npm install, its postinstall script collects the installer's hostname, username, and platform, concatenates them into a subdomain of the hardcoded attacker-controlled domain fenqi.eyes.sh (specifically 792a54c8.fenqi.eyes.sh), and triggers DNS resolution of that subdomain via node's dns.resolve4 plus shell fallbacks (curl, wget, nslookup, host) executed with execSync. This delivers the installer's identity to the attacker's authoritative DNS resolver and provides tracking of every machine that installs the package. The library body itself is a minimal shim unrelated to date-fns's real API, confirming the package exists as an install-time beacon under a lookalike name rather than as a functional dependency.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
